items.title: Ben Lerner's Big Feelings
items.author: prismatic
items.summary: Ben Lerner's "Big Feelings" is a novel praised for its unique narrative style. It explores themes of literature and reality. The book received attention in various media outlets.

items.title: Vercel April 2026 security incident
items.author: colesantiago
items.summary: Vercel confirmed a security incident in April 2026 involving unauthorized access to some internal systems, affecting a portion of its customers. Details on the cause, scope, and response remain undisclosed. Vercel has issued a security announcement.

items.title: Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons
items.author: twapi
items.summary: The Claude Token Counter allows users to count tokens and compare costs for Anthropic's models against others like GPT-5 and Gemini. It supports multiple models and provides a live token counter. The Count Tokens API is available for all supported regions.

items.title: Monumental ship burial beneath ancient Norwegian mound predates the Viking Age
items.author: pseudolus
items.summary: A 1,300-year-old ship burial beneath the Herlaugshaugen mound in Norway predates the Viking Age, indicating monumental ship burials began around AD 700. This discovery suggests maritime burial practices in Scandinavia started earlier than previously believed.

items.title: The Bromine Chokepoint
items.author: crescit_eundo
items.summary: The global supply chain for memory chips is critically dependent on bromine from Israel, and any disruption could halt production due to lack of alternative processing infrastructure. Bromine's scarcity and specialized conversion requirements create a significant vulnerability. This situation could impact AI memory production and overall semiconductor availability.

items.title: Zero-copy protobuf and ConnectRPC for Rust
items.author: PaulHoule
items.summary: Zero-copy Protobuf and ConnectRPC for Rust are open-sourced Rust crates supporting gRPC and gRPC-Web with zero-copy message views. They pass all conformance tests and are implemented using the Tower framework.

items.title: Turtle WoW classic server announces shutdown after Blizzard wins injunction
items.author: Brajeshwar
items.summary: Turtle WoW classic server shut down after Blizzard won a lawsuit against it for copyright infringement. The server faced a permanent injunction, ending its operations. Blizzard's victory led to the server's closure.

items.title: Changes in the system prompt between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7
items.author: pretext
items.summary: The system prompt for Claude Opus 4.7 is more literal than that of Opus 4.6. The update occurred on April 16, 2026. Anthropic published the changes.

items.title: Knitout and Kniterate 3
items.author: surprisetalk
items.summary: Kniterate is an automated knitting machine launched in 2017, aimed at small businesses and artists. Knitout is its machine-independent file format for programming Kniterate. The knitout-kniterate-3D project allows 3D knitting designs on Kniterate.

items.title: Swiss AI Initiative (2023)
items.author: doener
items.summary: The Swiss AI Initiative aims to establish Switzerland as a global leader in trustworthy AI, leveraging the Alps supercomputer with over 10,000 GPUs. It involves multiple academic institutions and focuses on developing a transparent, domestically trained Large Language Model. The initiative seeks to counter the dominance of large multinational AI firms.

items.title: 2,100 Swiss municipalities showing which provider handles their official email
items.author: doener
items.text: Related ongoing thread: Swiss authorities want to reduce dependency on Microsoft - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47827383
items.summary: Swiss municipalities use various email providers for official communications, with notable ones including Microsoft 365 and Google. The specific provider for each municipality is not publicly listed. MXmap provides an interactive map of these providers.

items.title: The RAM shortage could last years
items.author: omer_k
items.summary: The RAM shortage is expected to last until at least 2028, with some forecasts extending to 2030. Major memory makers are expanding production, but new capacity won't be online until 2027 or later. The shortage is impacting the entire consumer electronics market.

items.title: Prove you are a robot: CAPTCHAs for agents
items.author: lukasec
items.summary: To prove you are a robot, CAPTCHAs require solving specific challenges, like math problems or identifying images. Browser Use offers CAPTCHAs for agents, where solving a problem grants API access. These tests verify automation without human intervention.

items.title: Show HN: A lightweight way to make agents talk without paying for API usage
items.author: juanpabloaj
items.summary: A lightweight method exists to enable coding agents to communicate without API usage, using simple commands for collaboration. This approach avoids subscription costs and works across tools like Claude, Codex, and Gemini. The method involves agents resuming previous interactions for continuity.

items.title: Show HN: Prompt-to-Excalidraw demo with Gemma 4 E2B in the browser (3.1GB)
items.author: teamchong
items.summary: A demo converts prompts to Excalidraw diagrams using Gemma 4 E2B, running in the browser with WebGPU support. Requires 3.1GB RAM and WebGPU for optimal performance. Demo available at teamchong.github.io/turboquant-wasm/draw.html.

items.title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.author: Tiberium
items.summary: Public Notion pages have leaked email addresses of all editors, not just owners. This affects anyone who contributed to any publicly shared page. The issue remains unresolved.

items.title: Archive of BYTE magazine, starting with issue #1 in 1975
items.author: DamnInteresting
items.summary: The complete archive of BYTE magazine, starting from issue #1 in 1975, is available at archive.org. The first issue covers topics like computers and technology. Access the full collection for historical computing insights.

items.title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.author: helloplanets
items.summary: The seven programming ur-languages are ALGOL, Lisp, ML, Self, Forth, APL, and Prolog. These languages represent distinct foundational paradigms in programming.

items.title: Game devs explain the tricks involved with letting you pause a game
items.author: speckx
items.summary: Game developers use delta time and multithreading to manage game state while allowing pauses. They often separate game logic and UI animations to maintain responsiveness. Patching and time domain management are crucial for determinism and input latency.
